reasoning: The benchmark price relies on the December 19 close, which saw a stark drop from ~246 to 239.37. Setting the threshold at a relative local low creates an elevated probability of subsequent prices exceeding it, largely due to typical mean-reversion and historical positive seasonal drift moving into late December. The built-in statistical tool estimates a 0.58 probability for the short term, which serves as a highly reliable anchor. For the subsequent resolution dates, general market variance begins to overshadow the immediate local threshold advantage. The January 20 date importantly precedes the upcoming January 21 ex-dividend date ($1.20 payout), allowing it to retain some artificial premium, while the March 21 date will have absorbed that payout drop. Adhering to the rule that predictive uncertainty should increase with longer time horizons, the estimates strictly taper down toward 0.50 as the dates get further out.
